title: NCLH Stock Sails 76% Higher This Year, Ahead Of Norwegian Cruise Line Earnings
text: Norwegian Cruise Line earnings are on tap, with actual profits expected amid a post-Covid recovery. NCLH stock has soared in 2023.

title: 25 S&P 500 Stocks to Target Next Month
text: Subscribers to  Chart of the Week  received this commentary on Sunday, July 23.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings: A Potential Value Trap?
text: On July 27, 2023, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd ( NCLH , Financial) saw a daily gain of 5.79%, with a Loss Per Share of 3.44. This raises the question: is the company's stock a potential value trap?

title: Solid Bookings to Aid Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Q2 Earnings
text: Norwegian Cruise's (NCLH) second-quarter top line is likely to have benefited from strong booking activities, occupancy improvements and fleet-expansion efforts.

title: Consumer Confidence Rebounds on Cooling Inflation: 5 Winners
text: Consumer confidence is rebounding on easing inflation, helping stocks like OneSpaWorld Holdings (OSW), Marriott International (MAR), Lifetime Brands (LCUT), Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) and Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH).

title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
text: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) possesses the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely earnings beat in its upcoming report. Get prepared with the key expectations.

title: 4 Discretionary Stocks to Buy as Inflation Continues to Ease
text: Stocks have been on a rally ever since the June inflation data showed signs of cooling.

title: 4 Discretionary Stocks to Buy as Inflation Continues to Ease
text: Inflation is showing signs of cooling, raising hopes that the Fed might soon end its interest rate hike policy. This is likely to help stocks like Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL), Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH), Acushnet (GOLF) and Lifetime Brands (LCUT).

title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings to Hold Conference Call on Second Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: MIAMI, July 20, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE: NCLH) (together with NCL Corporation Ltd., “Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings” or the “Company”) announced today it will report second quarter 2023 financial results on Tuesday, August 1, 2023 at 7:30 a.m. Eastern Time with a conference call and webcast to discuss results at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Recently Broke Out Above the 20-Day Moving Average
text: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) reached a significant support level, and could be a good pick for investors from a technical perspective. Recently, NCLH broke through the 20-day moving average, which suggests a short-term bullish trend.

title: Carnival Cruise Lines: Buy, Sell, or Hold?
text: Cruise line stocks have been big winners this year, and their near-term outlooks have never been brighter. Carnival had to jack up its debt levels and share count to get through the pandemic, but it's paying off now.

title: Norwegian Cruise's 'Massive' Gains Propel a Downgrade
text: Norwegian Cruise Lines stock is up 79% this year. Truist Securities thinks more gains are baked into shares.

title: Norwegian Cruise stock falls as analyst worries the ‘undeniable' recovery is already priced in
text: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. was downgraded by Truist analyst C.

title: What tech boom? A homebuilder, 3 cruise lines, and Chipotle have all outperformed Apple and Microsoft on the S&P 500 this year
text: The tech stock boom has some of its thunder stolen by outperformers from other industries.  A homebuilder stock, three cruise operators, and Chipotle have all outperformed Apple and Netflix on the S&P 500 this year.

title: Op-ed: Cruise line stocks are a light on the horizon for investors seeking diversification
text: Market breadth improved as of late May and leadership expanded to cyclical sectors. One place for investors seeking diversification could be cruise companies.

title: This Year's Most Buoyant Sector Was a Win for Value Investors
text: Cruise lines were the top performer of the second quarter and among the top U.S. stocks for the year. Their secret was low expectations.

title: Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Hits 52-Week High: What's Aiding It?
text: Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) benefits from a solid booking trend, backed by an increase in consumer demand and encouraging fleet expansion initiatives.

title: Why Cruise Line Stocks Surged Between 28% and 68% in June
text: Wall Street analysts became incrementally bullish on the cruise sector last month. Carnival also reported earnings in June, with a positive bookings outlook and plans to pay down debt.

title: Will Consumer Discretionary Continue Defying The Doubters In Q3?
text: Most investors are pretty up-to-speed on how the AI boom contributed to supersized gains in the tech sector in the second quarter. Heavily-weighted stocks such as Apple Inc. NASDAQ: AAPL, Microsoft Corp. NASDAQ: MSFT, Nvidia Corp. NASDAQ: NVDA, Broadcom Inc. NASDAQ: AVGO and Adobe Inc. NASDAQ: ADBE posted double-digit price increases.

title: Carnival Cruise Lines Is Up 133% This Year. Just How High Can It Go?
text: Cruise stocks have surged in 2023 as they begin to dig themselves out of their pandemic and inflationary debt holes. Despite more than doubling this year, Carnival stock is still 75% below all-time highs.
